rulings.Case Title: WINSTON S NICHOLS ET AL VS. ASBESTOS COMPANIES
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Defendant Gardner Denver, Inc.S Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication As To Plaintiffs Request For Punitive Damages
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, April 25, 2018 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Gardner Denver, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Plaintiffs concede that defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiffs failed to submit admissible evidence to create a triable issue whether Mr. Nichols was exposed to any products attributable to defendant that were removed from and/or installed on a Gardner Denver pump. Mr. Keef's statements in his declaration regarding the supplier of the gaskets contradict his deposition testimony and were not considered. Further, even assuming that plaintiffs submitted evidence sufficient to create a triable issue whether Mr. Nichols was exposed to gaskets for which defendant is responsible, plaintiffs failed to submit admissible evidence to create a triable issue whether those gaskets contained asbestos. Defendant's objections to the declaration of Donald Keef are sustained as to paragraphs 10, 11 and 12 (solely regarding the plural use of "pumps"), sustained as to paragraphs 24, 27 and 29 (second sentence) and the remainder of defendant's objections are overruled. Defendant's objections to the declaration of Philip John Templin are sustained as to paragraphs 31, 32, 38 and 39 and the remainder of defendant's objections are overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m., Hon. Lynn O'Malley-Taylor presiding. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/LOT)
